title: Iran flows steady, Venezuela exports lag
section_path: Iran flows steady, Venezuela exports lag
section_path_text: Iran flows steady, Venezuela exports lag
summary:
Main
Iran exports steady near 1.2mbd; Venezuela lags at 540kbd on diluent shortages. Sanctioned flows hold but hinge on China demand, deep discounts. By Claire Jungman
Iran's crude exports have averaged around 1.2mbd in the first half of August 2025 - roughly matching mid-August 2024 levels.

children.title: Main
children.summary: Iran exports steady near 1.2mbd; Venezuela lags at 540kbd on diluent shortages. Sanctioned flows hold but hinge on China demand, deep discounts.

children.title: Methodology comments: Bigger is not always better
section_path: Methodology comments: Bigger is not always better
children.section_path_text: Methodology comments: Bigger is not always better
children.summary: While some market reports may publish higher Iranian export figures, bigger is not necessarily more accurate. Differences often come down to methodology.

children.title: Venezuela exports fall on diluent shortages
section_path: Venezuela exports fall on diluent shortages
children.section_path_text: Venezuela exports fall on diluent shortages
children.summary: Venezuela's crude exports have averaged 540kbd in the first half of August 2025, markedly lower than the 660kbd shipped over the same period in August 2024. This shortfall underscores how critical diluents-lighter hydrocarbons used for blending heavy crude-have become to sustaining exports.

children.title: Forward Look
children.summary:
The mid-August export picture from Iran and Venezuela reinforces a broader theme: sanctioned crude has proven resilient, but not without costs. Resilient but pressured: Flows remain a stable component of global balances, yet current Venezuelan weakness and Iran's fluctuations show that sanctions continue to exert pressure - especially when combined with logistical chokepoints such as diluent shortages
China as the anchor: Demand is increasingly concentrated in a single market.
